assessor_1_raw: Dense retrieval vs. BM25: methodologically sound. Strict label, weak-label audit, temporal split, and comprehensive secondary metrics are all appropriate. Coverage and popularity correlation are excellent diagnostics. Verdict: defense_wins.
assessor_2_raw: Well-designed retrieval experiment. Hard negative mining from BM25 is standard practice. Per-product-area NDCG@5 enables detailed confound analysis. Manual audit of dense wins vs. BM25 is qualitative validation. Test set policy is rigorous. Verdict: defense_wins.
assessor_3_raw: The design correctly handles the major risks in retrieval evaluation: popularity bias (strict label + Spearman correlation), temporal drift (monthly test breakdown), coverage (dedicated metric), and template leakage (stratification by query type). No methodological flaws. Verdict: defense_wins.
adjudication_raw: Unanimous defense_wins from all three assessors across all runs.
